hero.title: Passionate Software Developer and Science Fiction Nerd

projects.title: My Experience as a Software Developer
projects.subtitle: // TECHNOLOGY STACK & PROJECTS

wintools.title: Windows Desktop Tools
wintools.description: At my current employer I developed numerous tools that extended an in-house on-premise legacy software with modern features. These include the ability to automatically place QR codes for invoices and other documents inside Microsoft Word files, as well as a communication interface between both applications for data exchange. As a private hobby project I additionally built a desktop database tool with a Windows Forms UI that visualizes an underlying SQLite database, displays statistics based on the data, and allows easy creation, editing, and deletion of records.
wintools.details: These projects taught me how to pragmatically modernize legacy systems through external tooling without touching the core application. Building the communication interface gave me a deep understanding of inter-process communication and data serialization. The standalone database project significantly strengthened my skills in UI development with Windows Forms and working with SQLite.

legacyweb.title: Independent Maintenance of Legacy Web Applications
legacyweb.description: At my current employer I am solely responsible for maintaining various intranet web applications that were developed before I joined. Over the years I have fixed numerous bugs and extended the applications with features requested by customers. Since these are on-premise web applications, I have additionally installed the web environments on customer servers at various client sites, including the associated infrastructure and configuration.
legacyweb.details: This work taught me how to quickly navigate unfamiliar, organically grown codebases and apply changes with minimal risk. Being solely responsible for these applications deepened my understanding of the full lifecycle of a web application — from development through to customer deployment. I learned to prioritize pragmatically and deliver stable solutions for production systems.

saas.title: Modern Web Development as Software as a Service in the Cloud
saas.description: Greenfield development of our in-house software at my current employer, gradually migrating existing on-premise applications to the cloud as Software-as-a-Service. Initially contributed to the backend as a full-stack developer using Java and Spring. After an internal team split into front- and backend, I specialized in frontend development with Angular, leveraging my existing solid knowledge of HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. Throughout development I worked closely with the entire team to align API concepts so that frontend and backend development go hand in hand. The application was progressively covered with Jest unit tests and continuously optimized through agile processes. Example features include web mailboxes, email sending, email template creation with interactive text modules, and global services provided via a shared library. Successfully performed version upgrades from Angular 19 through 22 and continuously monitored and resolved CVEs.
saas.details: This project showed me how critical close collaboration between front- and backend teams is when designing APIs. I learned to build scalable and maintainable Angular applications with state management, unit tests, and reusable libraries. The regular version upgrades and CVE remediation significantly sharpened my awareness of security and long-term software maintainability.

ggdb.title: Personal Full-Stack Web Application
ggdb.description: As a passionate gamer, I developed a web application that lets you track which games you have completed and how much fun you had — including a full rating system. The application offers numerous customization options such as a theme picker as well as optional features like time tracking. The entire development process — from planning and conception through frontend development with Angular and backend development with PHP and MySQL, to deployment on my own server and version control — was entirely my responsibility. Statistics and various views are generated from the tracked games, allowing you to follow your annual gaming progress. Every update is accompanied by carefully maintained patch notes. The application is currently not public but invite-only. A free, public version is planned for the future. Feel free to contact me by email at any time — I am happy to provide a demo account.
ggdb.details: This project gave me comprehensive experience in independently planning and delivering a complete web application for multiple users. I was responsible for every aspect — from database modeling and REST API design to deployment. Particularly valuable was the experience of operating, maintaining, and iteratively evolving an application over the long term.

gamedev.title: Game Development
gamedev.description: Conception and prototyping of original computer games. Completed various smaller projects with Unreal Engine, Unity, and Godot 4 to get a feel for each engine. Along the way I made smaller asset adjustments, for example using Blender. The accumulated experience allowed me to make a well-informed decision about which engine suits a larger game project best. I am currently developing my first original game with Godot 4, for which I have already created a concept and prototype, and which I continuously develop as a hobby project.
gamedev.details: Working with multiple game engines gave me a broad understanding of different development philosophies — from Unreal's visual Blueprint system and Unity's component-based architecture to Godot's lean, scene-based approach. I learned how to design game mechanics, rapidly prototype ideas, and create assets with Blender and Aseprite. This experience helps me make well-founded technical decisions in game development.

agile.title: Agile Software Development
agile.description: Both at my employer and in my personal projects, I place great value on agile development. In the team, Daily Standups allow us to quickly identify blockers and resolve them efficiently. Daily Checkouts help maintain a clear overview of the overall development progress. In regular retrospectives we discuss team-internal as well as cross-company processes and derive actionable items for continuous improvement. In my private solo projects I apply the same principles to my own workflow: I plan in iterations, track progress and open points in structured notes, and regularly reflect on what is working well and what I can optimize — without a team, but with the same discipline.
agile.details: Agile methods have shown me that regular communication and structured reflection are the key to sustainable progress — whether in a team or working alone. I learned how to identify blockers early, communicate priorities clearly, and improve processes iteratively. Applying agile principles to solo projects has also helped me work in a more disciplined and focused way.

aidev.title: AI-Assisted Development
aidev.description: For about a year I have been increasingly using AI-assisted tools in web development at my current employer, witnessing first-hand the rapidly improving quality of available models. I actively follow current developments and integrate AI more and more into my daily professional workflow. In doing so I have learned how to develop agentically, how to improve prompts through structured markdown documents, and how to set up and configure MCP servers. Throughout this I maintain a critical eye on the actual AI output to meaningfully evaluate results and optimize them accordingly.
aidev.details: Working with AI tools has shown me how important it is to critically question outputs rather than accepting them blindly. I learned how precise prompting and well-structured context documents can significantly improve the quality of AI-generated results. Experience with agentic development and MCP servers has given me a deep understanding of the possibilities and limitations of current AI tools in professional software development.
